double_ratchet.DHs: This party's Diffie-Hellman ratchet key pair (private + public). The public part is sent in headers, while the private part is used for DH shared secret computations. It rotates on DH ratchet steps for forward secrecy.
double_ratchet.DHr: The latest remote party Diffie-Hellman ratchet public key received from the peer. It is combined with the local DHs private key to derive fresh secrets.
double_ratchet.RK: Root key for the Double Ratchet. It's the result of the shared secret computation using the DHr and DHs keys. It evolves during DH ratchet updates and is used to derive new sending and receiving chain keys.
double_ratchet.CKs: Sending chain key. It advances for each outgoing message and derives a one-time message key for encryption.
double_ratchet.CKr: Receiving chain key. It advances for each incoming message and derives the matching message key for decryption.
double_ratchet.Ns: Number of messages sent in the current sending chain.
double_ratchet.Nr: Number of messages received in the current receiving chain.
double_ratchet.PN: Length of the previous sending chain before the last DH ratchet step.
double_ratchet.MKSKIPPED: Storage for skipped message keys used to decrypt out-of-order messages. Entries are indexed by remote DH key and message number.
double_ratchet.header_dh: Header DH ratchet public key attached to this message. It identifies the sender ratchet step and is public metadata.
double_ratchet.header_pn: Header PN (previous chain length). It tells how many messages were sent in the sender's previous sending chain.
double_ratchet.header_n: Header message number N in the current sending chain. It is public metadata used for ordering and skipped-key handling.
double_ratchet.step_viz_plaintext: The raw user message in plaintext before any encryption occurs. The double ratchet algorithm ensures this plaintext is protected using authenticated encryption with associated data (AEAD) and a fresh message key.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sender_before_dhs: The sender's current local Diffie-Hellman (DH) public key before processing the message. This public key corresponds to the private key used in the current DH ratchet epoch to compute the root key.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sender_before_pn: The previous sending chain length (PN). It represents the number of messages sent in the previous sending chain before the last DH ratchet step occurred.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sender_before_ns: The current sending message number (Ns) before this send operation. It serves as an index for the message within the current sending chain.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sender_before_cks: The current sending chain key (CKs) before the symmetric ratchet step. This key is fed into the Key Derivation Function (KDF) to generate the message key and the next chain key.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sender_before_panel: A snapshot of the sender's party state before processing the outgoing message. This state includes the current DH ratchet key, chain counters, and symmetric chain key that will be evolved during the send step.
double_ratchet.step_viz_send_chain_cks: The current sending chain key used as the input for the symmetric-key ratchet step. It ensures forward secrecy for the symmetric chain by irreversibly deriving the next state.
double_ratchet.step_viz_kdf_ck: The symmetric-key Key Derivation Function (KDF). It takes the current sending chain key and derives two new keys: the next sending chain key (for future messages) and a one-time message key (for encrypting this specific message).
double_ratchet.step_viz_new_cks: The newly derived sending chain key that will replace the old one in the sender's state. It will be used as the input for the KDF when sending the next message.
double_ratchet.step_viz_message_key: The derived one-time message key (MK) used exclusively for encrypting this particular message. Once used, it is discarded to maintain forward secrecy.
double_ratchet.step_viz_state_update: The sender's state update after the symmetric send-chain step. The sending chain key (CKs) is irrevocably rotated to a fresh key, and the sending message number (Ns) is incremented by 1.
double_ratchet.step_viz_encrypt_mk: The one-time message key (MK) derived in the previous step, specifically assigned for the authenticated encryption of this plaintext.
double_ratchet.step_viz_encrypt_plaintext: The user's plaintext message that will be passed into the authenticated encryption algorithm.
double_ratchet.step_viz_ad_header: The Concatenated Associated Data (AD) and the message header. This data is authenticated but not encrypted, ensuring that the message is bound to its header and context and cannot be tampered with.
double_ratchet.step_viz_encrypt_fn: The Authenticated Encryption with Associated Data (AEAD) algorithm. It securely encrypts the plaintext using the message key while authenticating both the plaintext and the associated data.
double_ratchet.step_viz_ciphertext: The resulting ciphertext produced by the AEAD encryption process. This encrypted payload is safe to be transmitted over the network.
double_ratchet.step_viz_header_dhs: The sender's current local Diffie-Hellman (DH) public key, which is included in the header. The receiver will use it to determine if a DH ratchet step is necessary.
double_ratchet.step_viz_header_pn: The previous sending chain length (PN) placed in the header. The receiver uses this to determine if any messages from the preceding chain were missed and need to be skipped and cached.
double_ratchet.step_viz_header_n: The current message number (N) placed in the header. The receiver uses this to properly order incoming messages and handle out-of-order delivery.
double_ratchet.step_viz_header_fn: The process of assembling the message header, which combines the sender's DH public key, the previous chain length (PN), and the current message number (N) into a serialized format.
double_ratchet.step_viz_header_output: The finalized header that is attached to the outgoing ciphertext. This public metadata provides the receiver with the necessary routing and ratchet state information.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sender_after_cks: The updated sending chain key (CKs) saved in the sender's state after this send operation. It will be used for the next outgoing message in the current epoch.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sender_after_ns: The incremented sending message counter (Ns) after the send operation, reflecting that a new message has been processed in this chain.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sender_after_panel: A snapshot of the sender's final state after successfully processing and queuing the outgoing message. The chain key and counter have been advanced.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sent_before_dhs: The sender's local DH public key before the message was sent. It remains mostly static until a DH ratchet occurs.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sent_before_pn: The previous chain length (PN) before the message was sent.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sent_before_ns: The message counter (Ns) before the message was sent.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sent_before_cks: The sending chain key (CKs) before the message was sent.
double_ratchet.step_viz_pending_queue: The encrypted message is now queued in the system and waiting to be retrieved by the receiving party. It contains the header and the ciphertext.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sent_after_dhs: The sender's local DH public key after sending. It remains unchanged during the symmetric ratchet step and will only change upon a DH ratchet step.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sent_after_pn: The previous chain length (PN) after sending. It remains unchanged until the next DH ratchet is initiated.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sent_after_ns: The message counter (Ns) after sending, which has been incremented to reflect the newly sent message.
double_ratchet.step_viz_sent_after_cks: The updated sending chain key (CKs) after sending, advanced via the symmetric KDF.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_header: The parsed header from the incoming message. It contains the sender's DH ratchet public key and message counters (PN, N) essential for the receiver's state updates.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_ciphertext: The encrypted payload received from the sender, waiting to be decrypted using the derived message key.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_before_dhr: The receiver's currently stored remote Diffie-Hellman public key (DHr) before processing this incoming message. This will be compared against the header's DH key.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_before_rk: The root key (RK) before handling this message. It is used to spin up new sending and receiving chain keys during a DH ratchet step.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_before_ckr: The receiving chain key (CKr) before this step. It will be advanced symmetrically to derive the message key needed for decryption.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_before_nr: The number of messages already received (Nr) in the current receiving chain before processing this new message.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_before_panel: A snapshot of the receiver's state before message processing. It includes the stored remote public key, root key, receiving chain key, and counters.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_skipped_lookup: The lookup identifier formed by the message's header DH key and message number N. It is used to query the MKSKIPPED cache for a previously skipped message key.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_skipped_check: The process of checking whether a message key for this specific, out-of-order message was already derived and stored in the skipped keys cache (MKSKIPPED).
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_skipped_path: The control flow branch taken based on the skipped check. If the key is found, the receiver skips the ratchet steps and decrypts immediately; otherwise, it proceeds with standard ratchet processing.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_fast_forward: When the incoming message's number (N) is greater than the receiver's counter's (Nr) next value, the receiver must fast-forward its symmetric receiving chain. It repeatedly applies the KDF to advance CKr and Nr, deriving missed message keys and storing them in MKSKIPPED until reaching the target message index.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_complete_old_chain: Before switching to the new DH ratchet epoch, the receiver checks whether all messages from the sender's previous sending chain have been received. The sender indicates how many messages it sent in that chain via header.pn. If the receiver's Nr has not reached that count, some messages are still in-transit. The receiving chain is fast-forwarded through the missing indices, and the derived message keys are saved in MKSKIPPED so those messages can still be decrypted when they arrive out of order.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_header_dh: The Diffie-Hellman public key extracted from the incoming message's header. It marks the sender's current DH ratchet epoch.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_compare_dh: The comparison between the incoming header's DH public key and the receiver's stored remote public key (DHr). A difference signals that the sender has rotated their DH key, necessitating a DH ratchet step on the receiver side.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_ratchet_decision: The branch decision based on the DH key comparison. The receiver will either continue deriving keys within the current symmetric chain, or perform a full DH ratchet step to generate fresh chain keys.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_ckr: The current receiving chain key (CKr) used as the input for the symmetric-key receive ratchet step.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_kdf_ck: The symmetric-key Key Derivation Function (KDF) for the receiving chain. It predictably derives the next receiving chain key and the one-time message key corresponding to the incoming message.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_new_ckr: The newly derived receiving chain key that updates the receiver's state, replacing the previous chain key.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_message_key: The derived one-time message key needed to specifically decrypt the current incoming ciphertext.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_decrypt_cipher: The received ciphertext that will be inputted into the authenticated decryption algorithm.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_decrypt_ad: The associated data (AD) bound with the header. It is provided to the decryption algorithm to verify that the unencrypted metadata has not been altered.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_decrypt_fn: The Authenticated Encryption with Associated Data (AEAD) algorithm securely decrypts the ciphertext using the message key and authenticates the associated data. Failure here implies corruption or mismatched keys.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_plaintext: The successfully recovered plaintext message after the authenticated decryption passes securely.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_after_dhr: The stored remote public DH key (DHr) on the receiver side after processing this message. If a DH ratchet step occurred, this key has been updated to match the header's DH key.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_after_rk: The root key (RK) after processing. It is updated only if a DH ratchet step took place to derive fresh chain keys.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_after_ckr: The updated receiving chain key (CKr) after the symmetric KDF step. It is now ready for the next incoming message in this chain.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_after_nr: The updated received-message counter (Nr) after successfully processing the incoming message.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_delivered: The decrypted, authenticated message is finalized and delivered to the recipient's application layer, and logged in the chat history.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_before_dhs: The receiver's local Diffie-Hellman public key (DHs) prior to the DH ratchet step. This key is about to be rotated.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_before_cks: The sender's sending chain key (CKs) on the receiver's side before the DH ratchet step, which is about to be reset for a new sending chain.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_before_pn: The previous-chain length value (PN) before the DH ratchet. It will be updated with the final count of the previous sending chain.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_before_ns: The sending counter (Ns) before the DH ratchet step, which currently tracks messages sent in the old chain.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_after_dhs: The receiver's freshly generated local DH key pair (DHs). This new key provides forward secrecy for the newly established epoch and will be sent in the next outgoing message headers.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_after_cks: The new sending chain key (CKs) outputted by the DH ratchet step, providing a fresh symmetric ratchet foundation for future outgoing messages.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_after_pn: The updated previous-chain length (PN), reflecting the total number of messages the receiver sent during the previous epoch before this DH ratchet step.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_after_ns: The sending counter (Ns) reset back to 0, marking the beginning of a fresh sending chain within the new epoch.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_dh_ratchet_apply: The comprehensive Diffie-Hellman ratchet transition process: it updates the remote DH key (DHr) from the header, derives a fresh Root Key (RK) and Receiving Chain Key (CKr), generates a new local key pair (DHs), derives a new Root Key (RK) and Sending Chain Key (CKs), and formally resets the message counters to launch the new epoch.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_set_dhr: The receiver replaces its stored remote public DH key (DHr) with the DH key extracted from the incoming message header. This assignment marks the beginning of the new ratchet epoch and will serve as the input to the first KDF_RK call.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_kdf_rk_1: The first KDF_RK call in this DH ratchet step. A Diffie-Hellman computation is performed using the new header DH public key and the receiver's current private DH key. The resulting shared secret, together with the current Root Key (RK), is fed into the KDF to produce a fresh Root Key and the new Receiving Chain Key (CKr).
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_set_nr_zero: The receiving message counter (Nr) is reset to 0, marking the start of the new receiving chain in this epoch. Every subsequent incoming message will be counted from index 0 within this new chain.
double_ratchet.step_viz_receive_kdf_rk_2: The second KDF_RK call in this DH ratchet step. A Diffie-Hellman computation is performed using the same header DH public key and the receiver's freshly generated private DH key. The resulting shared secret, combined with the Root Key produced by KDF_RK #1, yields a new Root Key and the Sending Chain Key (CKs) for future outgoing messages.
